title: Ensure user customer secret keys rotate every 90 days

input_row.title: Ensure user customer secret keys rotate every 90 days
input_row.description: Object Storage provides an API to enable interoperability with Amazon S3. To use this Amazon S3 Compatibility API, you need to generate the signing key required to authenticate with Amazon S3. This special signing key is an Access Key/Secret Key pair. Oracle generates the Customer Secret key to pair with the Access Key.
input_row.rationale: It is important to rotate customer secret keys at least every 90 days, as they provide the same level of object storage access that the user they are associated with has.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Login to OCI Console. 2. Select Identity & Security from the Services menu. 3. Select Domains from the Identity menu. 4. For each domain listed, click on the name and select Users. 5. Click on an individual user under the Username heading. 6. Click on Customer Secret Keys in the lower left-hand corner of the page. 7. Ensure the date of the Customer Secret Key under the Created column of the Customer Secret Key is no more than 90 days old.
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Login to OCI Console. 2. Select Identity & Security from the Services menu. 3. Select Domains from the Identity menu. 4. For each domain listed, click on the name and select Users. 5. Click on an individual user under the Username heading. 6. Click on Customer Secret Keys in the lower left-hand corner of the page. 7. Delete any Access Keys with a date older than 90 days under the Created column of the Customer Secret Keys.
input_row.additional_information: The Audit Procedure and Remediation Procedure for OCI IAM without Identity Domains can be found in the CIS OCI Foundation Benchmark 2.0.0 under the respective recommendations.
input_row.justification: The control requires checking the rotation of access keys, which can be automated using AWS IAM APIs. The second opinion correctly identifies that AWS IAM Access Keys can be managed and checked using the `boto3` library. This allows for programmatically listing access keys and verifying their creation dates, ensuring compliance with the 90-day rotation policy.
input_row.automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: IAM Users - API/CLI Method: `boto3.client('iam').list_access_keys(UserName='user_name')` - Properties to Check: `CreateDate` attribute of each access key - Pass Criteria: All access keys have a `CreateDate` within the last 90 days. - Fail Criteria: Any access key has a `CreateDate` older than 90 days.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** oci_iam_user_customer_secret_key_rotation_90_days

---

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **OCI APIs/SDK/CLI to Use:**
  - List identity domains: [`oci iam identity-domain list`](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/iam/identity-domain/list.html)
  - List users in domain: [`oci iam identity-domain-user list`](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/iam/identity-domain-user/list.html)
  - List customer secret keys for a user: [`oci iam user-customer-secret-key list`](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/iam/user-customer-secret-key/list.html)
  - [OCI Python SDK: `list_customer_secret_keys`](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/tools/python/latest/api/identity/client/oci.identity.IdentityClient.html#oci.identity.IdentityClient.list_customer_secret_keys)

- **Automated Steps:**
  1. Enumerate all identity domains in the tenancy.
  2. For each domain, enumerate all users.
  3. For each user, list all customer secret keys.
  4. For each customer secret key, check its `time_created` attribute.
  5. Verify that the key’s creation date is no more than 90 days old.

- **Pass Criteria:**
  - All active customer secret keys for all users in all domains have a creation date within the last 90 days.

- **Fail Criteria:**
  - Any customer secret key (that is not in a disabled/deleted state) was created more than 90 days ago.

- **Sample OCI CLI Command:**
  ```
  oci iam user-customer-secret-key list --user-id <user_ocid>
  ```
  - Inspect `time-created` in the output and compare to current date.

- **Python SDK Example:**
  ```python
  from oci.identity import IdentityClient
  import datetime

  # Assume proper config/session setup
  identity_client = IdentityClient(config)

  users = identity_client.list_users(compartment_id=<tenancy_ocid>).data

  for user in users:
      keys = identity_client.list_customer_secret_keys(user.id).data
      for key in keys:
          created_time = key.time_created
          age = (datetime.datetime.now(created_time.tzinfo) - created_time).days
          if age > 90:
              print(f"User {user.name} - Secret key {key.id} is {age} days old (over 90 days)!")
  ```

- **References:**
  - [OCI Documentation: Managing Customer Secret Keys](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/access/troubleshooting-secret-keys.htm)
  - [OCI API - List Customer Secret Keys](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/api/#/en/identity/20160918/CustomerSecretKeySummary/ListCustomerSecretKeys)
  - [CLI Reference: oci iam user-customer-secret-key list](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/iam/user-customer-secret-key/list.html)

---

**SOURCE:** CIS_Oracle_Cloud_Infrastructure_Foundations_Benchmark_v3_0_0
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification as "Automated" is correct. The use of OCI CLI and Python SDK to automate the audit of customer secret key rotation is appropriate. The task involves programmatically checking the age of secret keys, which is well-suited for automation.
- This cannot be checked via AWS APIs/boto3/CLI as it pertains specifically to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) and not AWS. The AWS SDK and CLI are not applicable here.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9
- The automation details are technically correct. The use of OCI CLI commands and Python SDK methods to list users and their customer secret keys is accurate.
- The sample OCI CLI command and Python SDK script are valid and demonstrate the correct logic for checking the age of secret keys.
- The program name `oci_iam_user_customer_secret_key_rotation_90_days` is descriptive and clear, following the convention for OCI-related checks. However, it could be slightly more descriptive by including "check" or "audit" to indicate the action being performed.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name could be improved to `oci_iam_user_customer_secret_key_rotation_check_90_days` to clearly indicate that it is a check or audit process.
- Consider adding error handling in the Python script to manage potential exceptions, such as network issues or API errors.
- It would be beneficial to include a mechanism to notify or log results for keys that are older than 90 days, rather than just printing to the console.
- Ensure that the script accounts for time zones correctly when calculating the age of the keys.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-structured, and the use of OCI CLI and Python SDK is appropriate for the task. The technical details are accurate, and the automation approach is sound.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to `oci_iam_user_customer_secret_key_rotation_check_90_days` for clarity.
- Consider adding error handling and logging mechanisms in the Python script for robustness.
- Overall, the assessment is solid and requires only minor improvements for clarity and completeness.
